mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses 20th-century architectural movements (Art Nouveau, Bauhaus, International Style, Brutalism, Postmodernism), new building materials like reinforced concrete and steel, and building types including the skyscraper, making it central to the history of Western architecture.

Vitruvius's De Architectura covers elements of architectural design (firmitas, utilitas, venustas), building materials, temple and domestic architecture, and is foundational to the history of Western architecture from Rome through the Renaissance.

Examines architecture's relationship to political power across history, including pyramids, Gothic cathedrals, Baroque palaces, and 20th-century state architecture, covering governmental, religious, and commemorative building types.

Angkor Wat exemplifies non-Western religious architecture with sophisticated constructional systems, bas-relief ornamentation, and water management engineering, representing Khmer architectural achievement.

Megalithic monuments represent primitive and folk architecture using stone construction, with Stonehenge, Avebury, and Carnac demonstrating early building techniques and social organisation for monumental construction.
The Grand Tour's influence on British Palladianism and garden design directly shaped 18th-century Western architectural and landscape design traditions.

Joseph Paxton's Crystal Palace pioneered revolutionary iron-and-glass architecture and influenced design reform, representing a landmark in 19th-century Western architectural history and commercial/exhibition architecture.
The Great Wall of China is a monumental example of military architecture across multiple dynasties, directly relevant to military fortification and non-Western architectural history.
Covers urban planning from Haussmann's Paris to modernist visions and the garden city movement, directly addressing the history of Western architecture and urban/landscape design.
The Vienna Secession Building by Joseph Maria Olbrich represents Art Nouveau/Jugendstil architectural ornamentation and design, connecting decorative arts to modernist architecture.
The Bauhaus school integrating fine art with architecture, typography, and furniture design profoundly influenced modernist architecture under Gropius and Mies van der Rohe.
Byzantine architecture including Hagia Sophia exemplifies the dome constructional system and religious architecture, representing a major chapter in Western architectural history.
Mughal architectural achievements including the Taj Mahal represent major non-Western religious and commemorative architecture.
Etruscan engineering achievements and their influence on Roman architecture (political institutions, construction) connect to early Western architectural history.
The Colosseum's architecture and engineering exemplify Roman constructional systems (arches, vaults) and recreational architecture (amphitheatres).
The 12th-century Renaissance's Gothic architectural revolution represents a major development in Western architectural history.
Safavid artistic flourishing in architecture, particularly Isfahan under Shah Abbas I, represents significant non-Western architectural achievement.

Sir John Soane's neoclassical architecture (Bank of England, Dulwich Picture Gallery) with innovative use of light and space represents significant Western architectural achievement and museum/gallery design.
